planSpec.content:
# Specification: Unified Agent Sessions & Chat View

## 1. Problem Statement

Currently, the Agent Sessions panel and the Chat panel are displayed as two separate windows/panels, creating a fragmented user experience. Additionally, there's a redundant "Deploy Scripts" section in the Agents tab that duplicates functionality already available in the top-right Agents indicator. The user wants a unified, cleaner interface where sessions and chat are combined into one cohesive view.

## 2. User Story

As a **user of DMaker**, I want **the Agent Sessions list and Chat view to be unified into a single cohesive panel**, so that **I can manage sessions and view conversations without navigating between separate windows, and I don't see redundant deploy script controls**.

## 3. Acceptance Criteria

### Happy Path
- **GIVEN** I am on the agent view, **WHEN** I select a session from the list, **THEN** the chat for that session displays in the same unified panel without opening a separate window
- **GIVEN** I am viewing the Agents panel (right side), **WHEN** I look at its contents, **THEN** I should NOT see the "Deploy Scripts" section

### Edge Cases
- **GIVEN** I have no active sessions, **WHEN** I view the unified panel, **THEN** I see an empty state encouraging me to create a new session
- **GIVEN** I have multiple sessions, **WHEN** I switch between them, **THEN** the chat content updates smoothly within the same panel

### Error Handling
- **GIVEN** a session fails to load, **WHEN** I select it, **THEN** an appropriate error message displays inline in the chat area

## 4. Technical Context

| Aspect | Value |
|--------|-------|
| Affected Files | `apps/ui/src/routes/agent.tsx`, `apps/ui/src/components/views/agent-view/`, `apps/ui/src/components/views/board-view/components/running-agents-panel.tsx`, `apps/ui/src/components/views/board-view/components/deploy-scripts-panel.tsx` |
| Dependencies | None (UI refactoring only) |
| Constraints | Must maintain existing session management functionality |
| Patterns to Follow | Existing panel patterns in board-view, Tailwind CSS styling conventions |

## 5. Non-Goals

- This does NOT change the top-right Agents indicator functionality
- This does NOT modify the actual agent/session backend logic
- This does NOT affect the Deploy settings page (only removing redundant panel)

## 6. Implementation Tasks

```tasks
## Phase 1: Analysis & Cleanup
- [ ] T001: Remove Deploy Scripts panel from Running Agents Panel | File: apps/ui/src/components/views/board-view/components/running-agents-panel.tsx
- [ ] T002: Analyze current agent-view structure to understand session/chat separation | File: apps/ui/src/routes/agent.tsx

## Phase 2: Unified Panel Implementation
- [ ] T003: Refactor agent view to combine sessions list and chat into unified layout | File: apps/ui/src/routes/agent.tsx
- [ ] T004: Update agent-view components to work with unified layout | File: apps/ui/src/components/views/agent-view/components/agent-header.tsx
- [ ] T005: Style the unified panel for a cohesive, polished appearance | File: apps/ui/src/routes/agent.tsx

## Phase 3: Cleanup & Polish
- [ ] T006: Remove any unused imports or dead code from refactoring | File: apps/ui/src/components/views/board-view/components/index.ts
- [ ] T007: Test and verify the unified view works correctly | File: apps/ui/src/routes/agent.tsx
```

## 7. Success Metrics

- Agent Sessions and Chat display as a single unified panel
- No "Deploy Scripts" section visible in the Agents panel (running-agents-panel)
- Clean, polished visual styling that matches existing design patterns
- All existing session management functionality preserved

## 8. Risks & Mitigations

| Risk | Mitigation |
|------|------------|
| Breaking existing session functionality | Preserve all existing hooks and state management, only change layout |
| Inconsistent styling | Follow existing Tailwind patterns and component styles |
| Missing edge cases | Test empty states, multiple sessions, and error states |

---

tasks.description: Remove Deploy Scripts panel from Running Agents Panel
Analyze current agent-view structure to understand session/chat separation
Refactor agent view to combine sessions list and chat into unified layout
Update agent-view components to work with unified layout
Style the unified panel for a cohesive, polished appearance
Remove any unused imports or dead code from refactoring
Test and verify the unified view works correctly
